draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from approachable, human-scaled navigation that feels like a trusted workspace rather than corporate software
facade.label: Warm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User values personality and comfort in their tools; design should use warm colors, soft spacing, and conversational language throughout the interface
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean efficiency and professional distance; design should lean cooler, more minimal, with neutral color palette and streamlined layout

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a CRM voice that feels like a helpful colleague rather than enterprise software
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Build conversational copy, approachable UI language, warm color palette, personality in error states and empty states
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into polished professionalism, minimal personality, clean corporate aesthetic, formal tone throughout

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRMs benefit from persistent left navigation + spacious card layouts for client overview
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence vs. content layout pairing
facade.label: Sidebar with card grid
facade.acceptImplies: User likely values quick client switching + visual scanability over dense information density
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based or list-heavy interfaces that prioritize sequential workflows or compact data display

evidence.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from approachable, human-scaled navigation that feels like a trusted workspace rather than corporate software
evidence.implication: User values personality and comfort in their tools; design should use warm colors, soft spacing, and conversational language throughout the interface

facade.hypothesis: Freelancer CRM should feel approachable and modern rather than corporate/rigid
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers friendly, softer aesthetics — likely values ease-of-use and modern SaaS styling over traditional business software look
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer cleaner minimalism or sharper definition — or finds rounded corners superficial; wants substance over softness

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer viewing client metrics (rates, project history, payment status) in compact tabular format rather than narrative summaries
facade.axisTargeted: Charts vs. Text — specifically, dense structured data vs. sparse card-based summaries
facade.label: Dense data table
facade.acceptImplies: Build CRM around queryable tables, sortable columns, inline editing; metrics-heavy dashboards; spreadsheet-like interface for power users
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into cards, timeline views, narrative summaries; metrics presented as key numbers or micro-charts; less data density per screen

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers snappy, instantaneous panel transitions (contacts, projects, invoices) over animated slide-ins that feel fluid but slower
facade.label: Instant open or smooth slide
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into instant state changes; modals and sidebars appear without animation; UI feels responsive and mechanical
facade.rejectImplies: Embrace easing curves and 200–400ms transitions; make every panel entry feel like a natural motion; prioritize felt smoothness over perceived speed

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with metrics/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals how much visibility into numbers they need to feel in control
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals preference for speed-focused vs. thoughtful interface rhythm

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with metrics/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals how much visibility into numbers they need to feel in control
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals preference for speed-focused vs. thoughtful interface rhythm
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A freelancer CRM should use gentle, desaturated palettes rather than bold brand colors to reduce visual fatigue during long workdays and create a calm, focused environment
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft, muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: The design should prioritize eye-rest and serenity; use grays, warm taupes, soft blues, and low-contrast UI elements throughout the interface
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer vibrant, high-contrast, or saturated color schemes that signal energy and positivity; the tool should feel stimulating rather than restful
